## Activate the workflow to make the MCP Trigger work
In order to make the MCP server available, you need to activate the workflow.

Then copy the Production URL of the MCP Trigger and paste it in the corresponding MCP Client tool.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
# Try these example requests with the AI Agent

### My Functions MCP
1. Use your tools to convert this text to lower case: `EXAMPLE TeXt`

2. Use your tools to convert this text to upper case: `example TeXt`

3. Generate 5 random user data, please.

4. Please obtain 3 jokes.




### Calendar MCP
5. What is my schedule for next week?

6. I have a meeting with John tomorrow at 2pm. Please add it to my Calendar.

7. Adjust the time of my meeting with John tomorrow from 2pm to 4pm, please.

8. Cancel my meeting with John, tomorrow.

If you are using n8n Cloud plans, it's very intuitive to setup and you may not even need the tutorial.

parameters.content:
## Why model 4o? 👆
After testing 4o-mini it had some difficulties handling the calendar requests, while the 4o model handled it with ease.

Depending on your prompt and tools, 4o-mini might be able to work well too, but it requires further testing.

parameters.content:
## Activate the workflow to make the MCP Trigger work
In order to make the MCP server available, you need to activate the workflow.

Then copy the Production URL of the MCP Trigger and paste it in the corresponding MCP Client tool.

## MCP Clients
For every tool here you need to obtain he corresponding Production URL from the MCP Triggers on the right 👉

# Try these example requests with the AI Agent

### My Functions MCP
1. Use your tools to convert this text to lower case: `EXAMPLE TeXt`

2. Use your tools to convert this text to upper case: `example TeXt`

3. Generate 5 random user data, please.

4. Please obtain 3 jokes.




### Calendar MCP
5. What is my schedule for next week?

6. I have a meeting with John tomorrow at 2pm. Please add it to my Calendar.

7. Adjust the time of my meeting with John tomorrow from 2pm to 4pm, please.

8. Cancel my meeting with John, tomorrow.

## Google Calendar tools require credentials
If you don't have your Google Credentials set up in n8n yet, watch [this]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} video to learn how to do it.

If you are using n8n Cloud plans, it's very intuitive to setup and you may not even need the tutorial.

# Author
![Solomon]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}
### Solomon
Freelance consultant from Brazil, specializing in automations and data analysis. I work with select clients, addressing their toughest projects.

Currently running the [Scrapes community]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} with Simon 💪

For business inquiries, email me at automations.solomon@gmail.com
Or message me on [Telegram]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} for a faster response.

## Check out my other templates
### 👉 {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## Why model 4o? 👆
After testing 4o-mini it had some difficulties handling the calendar requests, while the 4o model handled it with ease.

Depending on your prompt and tools, 4o-mini might be able to work well too, but it requires further testing.
# Learn How to Build an MCP Server and Client

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Calendar  O Auth2  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When Executed by Another Workflow".
Open the "AI Agent" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SearchEvent" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "CreateEvent" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "UpdateEvent" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "DeleteEvent" node (n8n-nodes-base.googleCalendarTool) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Calendar MCP" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Switch" node (n8n-nodes-base.switch) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert Text" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "When chat message received" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Simple Memory" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Calendar MCP" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "My Functions" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "My Functions Server" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Random user data" node (n8n-nodes-base.debugHelper) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Generate random user data" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Joke Request" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Random Jokes" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "OpenAI 4o" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note7" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note8" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Convert Text to Upper Case".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Convert Text to Lower Case".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Return only some fields".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
